repo_signals: L3 smoke for platform.service-mesh: sidecar injection, VirtualService DestinationRule traffic management, mTLS, retries timeouts circuit breaking, canary traffic split, golden signals, mesh overhead

retrieval_query: service mesh istio linkerd sidecar injection virtualservice destinationrule mtls retry timeout circuit breaker canary traffic split golden signals

validation_smoke.name: platform.service-mesh L3 validation smoke
signals: Injected pods show two containers and the proxy config dump lists the expected listeners and clusters
A traffic split sends the configured percentage to the canary subset, confirmed by per-subset request metrics
mTLS STRICT mode rejects plaintext calls and accepts mesh-internal calls with the issued workload certificates
An injected 5xx triggers the configured retry/circuit-breaker and the caller sees the outlier host ejected

diagnosis_smoke.name: platform.service-mesh diagnosis smoke
signals: Requests bypass mesh policy because the sidecar was not injected (namespace label or annotation missing)
A VirtualService route never matches because host/gateway/port fields don't line up with the DestinationRule subsets
mTLS connection failures traced to a PeerAuthentication STRICT mode without a matching DestinationRule trafficPolicy tls mode
Retries amplify load during an outage because retry budget/timeout interplay was not bounded
Latency regression attributed to per-hop proxy overhead and excessive telemetry rather than the application
